characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Almighty Janitor: A ragtag group of jewel miners, and not only Jollywood's most trusted heroes, but the descendants of the kingdom's founding fathers. Badass Crew: There's nothing to stop the Glooms (and other hostile villains) without the 7D. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: The trio into two pairs, while Dopey fits all of it, especially beauty.Happy is a happy-go-lucky soul (beauty), Doc is a gadget genius (brains), and Grumpy is a tough and hotheaded kind (brawn)Ziggzagged with the other trio: Sneezy is the brawn (and/or beauty), Sleepy is the brains (and/or brawn) and Bashful is the beauty (and/or brains). Happy is a happy-go-lucky soul (beauty), Doc is a gadget genius (brains), and Grumpy is a tough and hotheaded kind (brawn) Ziggzagged with the other trio: Sneezy is the brawn (and/or beauty), Sleepy is the brains (and/or brawn) and Bashful is the beauty (and/or brains).

characters.name: Happy
characters.description: Break the Cutie: Becomes close to tears andgoes a little madwhen his guitar is taken away in "The Enchanted Forest Ranger". Cartoon Conductor: Taken to a logical extreme in "The Jollywood Jam", where his conductor baton is accidentally swapped with Grim's new wand, magically putting his band in different costumes and settings. In the "Rock of Sages", he was also able to make a group of screaming hills sing with a stick as a baton. Casanova Wannabe: Or possiblyThe Casanova.Happy:By the way, ladies... I'm single! Catchphrase: “Whoopty doopty schmoodily doo!” Cute Mute: He was this in "Whose Voice Is It Anyway", where he gives his voice away to Echo Canyon so he can keep a bet to not sing for 24 hours. Usually, though, he's very loud and upbeat. He also became this in "You Ain't Seen Nothing Yeti" when Hildy took away his voice and turned him into a mime. The other dwarves had to get Dopey to translate.

characters.name: Sneezy
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: Second only to Grumpy in being the most outspoken of the dwarfs. A Day in the Limelight: "Sneezin' Season" where he's worried he might be allergic to Grumpy, and "The Big Bad Sneezy," whereThe Big Bad Wolftricks him into joining the "Huff'n'Puff Incorporated Demolition Firm," which specializes in blowing down pig's houses. The episode "Take Your Pet to Lunch Day" also focuses on his inexplicable animal allergies and him getting a hypoallergenic pet dragon. Disguised in Drag: Dresses up asLittle Red Riding Hoodto foolThe Big Bad Wolf, and Queen Delightful (together with Dopey) to fool the Glooms. Gag Nose: Considering his trait that stands out to him, Sneezy has the biggest nose of the 7D (if not the whole villagers). Meaningful Appearance: Sneezy has an appropriately large and runny red nose to reflect his allergies.

characters.name: Dopey
characters.description: Cute Mute: He can communicate through whistles, but doesn't talk. Even acknowledged in "Knick Knack Patty Whack" when they dress Dopey and Sleepy as a salesman, with Dopey as the top half. He's also able to translate for the other 5D when Happy is turned into a mime.Doc: I'll admit my plan had a few flaws.Grumpy: Like putting the one of us who doesn't talk on top? Yeah, that's a flaw. Fluffy Tamer: He can tame dragons and glitterpillars. Friend to All Living Things: He's very friendly and has a knack for animals. Apparently, he can communicate with them. Going Native: Dopey's the only one who didn't end up behind bars by Dirk Dumphrey, so he can summon wild animals to chase the forest ranger while setting the 6D free. Hidden Depths: Despite being called "Dopey" and being treated as more of a pet than a person, he has shown to be a good artist, pantomimer, beast tamer, and magician among other things.

characters.name: Bashful
characters.description: Actor Allusion: In "A Sneeze in Time", Bashful gets sent forward in time by 1000 years. Another one ofBilly West's characters,Philip J. Fry, gets himself frozen in time and reawakened in the future 1000 years later. Betty and Veronica: Gender-inverted. He is the Betty to Starchy's Veronica when both of them have a crush on Queen Delightful. Catchphrase: "Floom!" whenever anything goes wrong. Chameleon Camouflage: Bashful has the ability to blend in with his surroundings, due to hisShrinking Violetnature. Clingy Jealous Girl: Gender-inverted. Gets this way in "Buckets" when he offers to keep an eye on Queen Delightful, which is Lord Starchbottom's job. Not only did he insisted to watch over her, but also avoided himself from going to the haunted mansion with the 6D.Bashful: Beat it, bub. I already called dibs!

characters.name: Doc
characters.description: Adaptational Intelligence: Although he can be eccentric and as gullible as the rest of the dwarfs, he's much smarter than his counterpart, more resourceful and aGadgeteer Geniusto boot. Badass Bookworm: The smartest of the 7D who protects Jollywood alongside them. Badass in Distress: Held hostage by Dr. Jingleheimer until the other dwarfs arrive to save him. Bound and Gagged: When Bingo kidnaps him. Cloudcuckoolander: Subverted. While he is the strategic and focused leader of the 7D, Doc can be goofy and eccentric to the point he spills out a bunch of fun facts and trivia about everything he observes, especially with rainbows and elves.Playedcompletelystraight at one point when he got his smarty tooth taken out, making him an idioticManchildinstead of an enthusiasticallyMad Scientist.

characters.name: Sleepy
characters.description: Benevolent Boss: In "Bedknobs and Gloomsticks", Sleepy becomes this to assign the 6D to build a bed for Queen Delightful after her castle was infested with termites by the Glooms. Blue Is Heroic: Wears blue pajamas and is able to take down the Glooms without the help of the 6D. Cloudcuckoolander: Lampshaded by Grumpy several times, as Sleepy has Mr. Hicklepips as his "pet" for the annual Take Your Pet to Lunch Day. He later assigned Grumpy to saw a tree with Mr. Hicklepips, much to his irritation, in "Bedknobs and Gloomsticks". Comically Missing the Point: Is prone to this, perhaps because even when he's awake he's usually only half-there. Companion Cube: Has a stuffed toy named Mr. Hicklepips, and Sleepy thinks that he's a "living pet".

characters.name: Grumpy
characters.description: Attractive Bent-Gender: Comes off this way to a guard when disguised as Maid Marzipan in "Nicely Done and the 7D". Not so much to the Really Nice Guys, who think that Maid Marzipan has "really let herself go." Big Eater: Signs up for Knight School only for the All You Can Eat Buffet. And in "Starchy Takes a Break", he only agrees to be Starchbottom's assistant because Queen Delightful's food tastes good. Butt-Monkey: Grumpy is often the butt of many jokes. Chick Magnet: In the episode where everyone's turned into frogs, allthe witches, including the Frog Witch known asPrincess Prettyhead,all kissed him (and all refer to him as "the cute one"). The Comically Serious: The reason why he was chosen as the party clown for Queen Delightful's(actually Starchy's)surprise party in "Surprise!" is that his seriousness as a clown is what makes him so funny.

characters.name: Queen Delightful
characters.description: The optimistic and cheerful ruler of Jollywood. Adaptational Heroism: Most Snow White adaptations have the queen as theBig Bad. This queen is theBig Good.noteOf course, there is the fan-theory she's Snow White's real mother. Then again, the Evil Queen was originally Snow White's mother, so that trope applies even if it were true. All-Loving Heroine: As the optimistic queen herself, she's often very openhearted to others. Beauty Equals Goodness: She serves as the fairest in the land and is one of the heroes alongside the 7D. Berserk Button: You donotwant to come between her and her dog Sir Yipsalot.Queen Delightful:You can be naughty with me, you can be naughty with my kingdom,but you CANNOT BE NAUGHTY WHEN IT COMES TO MY DOG!

characters.name: Lord Starchbottom
characters.description: Queen Delightful's royal servant. Beleaguered Assistant: He's Queen Delightful's only staff member and doeseverythingaround the castle. Betty and Veronica: Gender-inverted. When it comes to Queen Delightful, Starchy is the Veronica to Bashful's Betty. Butt-Monkey: He's no different from Grumpy, but Starchy has a rather painful receiving end of this trope more often. Fake Aristocrat: He pretends to be royalty when in fact he's from a working-class background.

characters.name: Sir Yipsalot
characters.description: The excitable pet dog of Queen Delightful. Amplified Animal Aptitude: Has been shown to be a talented painter in "The Fairest in the Land", a talented dancer in "Bathtub Bashful", and even called upon the 7D (or Sleepy the 1D, in this case) when he realized something had gone wrong in "Sleepytime". Beware the Nice Ones: According to Delightful, if he's ever put in the wrong hands, he would react erratically, and both Hildy and Grim learn this the hard way. Ridiculously Cute Critter: A royal pet dog with an enthusiastic and loyal personality. Trademark Favorite Food: Pickles.

characters.name: Magic Mirror
characters.description: A sentient mirror that is located in the Jollywood royal palace. Alliterative Name:MagicMirror. Ink-Suit Actor: She's pretty much Whoopi Goldberg as aMagic Mirror. Magic Mirror: Obviously, she is. Out of Focus: Doesn't appear as often in season 2.

characters.name: Not-So-Magic Mirror
characters.description: Another magical mirror who is more blunt than pleasant. Brutal Honesty: He outright stated that Hildy is not the fairest of them all considering her envy towards better people, much to her chagrin. The Gadfly: He's the only magic mirror to be known as annoying with other magic mirrors, especially to the store seller and subsequently the Glooms. Ink-Suit Actor: The Not-So Magic Mirror resembles the voice actor who played him. One-Shot Character: He only appeared in "Mirror, Mirror".

characters.name: Buckets
characters.description: A ghost from a haunted mansion who plays the ukelele. Cute Ghost Girl: She’s a ghost, and she’s as cute as a button. Fog Feet: She has a genie-like ghostly tail instead of feet. Friendly Ghost: Buckets is aNice Girlwho means no harm or fear to anyone. Glowing Eyes of Doom: She gets these at the end of her lullaby to Grumpy. Subverted, as no one was in any danger at all.

characters.name: Hildy Gloom
characters.description: The main antagonist of the series, Hildy's a witch whose overall goal is to seize Queen Delightful's throne for her to become the new Queen of Jollywood. All Girls Want Bad Boys: There's a reason the equally mischievous Grim was her husband. Alpha Bitch: She's a quintessentialEvil Sorceresswho plans to usurp Jollywood's throne for her to become its new Queen. Arch-Enemy: Delightful and Snazzy Shazam are two of Hildy's biggest rivals. Baby Talk: Whenever she wants Grim to do something and feels like asking nicely.

characters.name: Grim Gloom
characters.description: Hildy's husband who also works as her magic-wielding right-hand man. He serves as the series' secondary antagonist. Affectionate Nickname: Hildy usually calls him "Grim" or "Grimmy", or even various sickening nicknames. Alliterative Name:GrimwoldGloom. Blue Is Calm: Has a blue head of hair and is more laid back than his wife. The Ditz: He's not particularly bright, much to Hildy's chagrin.

characters.name: Dirk Dumphrey
characters.description: A forest ranger for the Enchanted Forest who abuses his position by giving frivolous violations. Alliterative Name:DirkDumphrey. Antagonist Title: The episode "The Enchanted Forest Ranger" centers around his shady actions. Creepy Crossdresser: Listen to Dirk's dull ramblings and you'll discover that he likes to wear all sorts of skirts and does so when he dresses up as a waitress for another job. Hate Sink: Ho, boy, is this guy arealpiece of work. He pretends to be a forest ranger and abuses that power to harass the 7D nonstop. In fact, he incarcerates themjust for committing unnecessary violations.

characters.name: Snazzy Shazam
characters.description: A witch who desires Queen Delight's position. She is also Hildy's archnemesis besides Delight herself. Alliterative Name:SnazzyShazam. Ambiguous Situation: It's quite unclear why Snazzy joined the villain syndicate with the goal of getting rid of the 7D. Unlike The Big Bad Wolf and Finders Keepers being part of said syndicate out of revenge on Sneezy and Grumpy respectively, however,this implies that it's due to them disguising as beauty pageant judgeswhile trying to get Queen Delightful'sTricked-Out Shoesthat Hildy was wearing. Arch-Enemy: Hildy Gloom is her main adversary in the show. Beauty Is Bad: Snazzy may be one of the show's villains, but she's still on the prettier side if anything.

characters.name: Mama Dragon
characters.description: A female dragon who's been searching for her long-lost child. Heel–Face Turn: After reuniting with her real baby, she becomes nice and calm and joins the Jollywood cookout. Mama Bear: She is protective of her baby. Mistaken for Related: She believed Grim (who was turned into a small dragon) for her lost baby after her egg was unintentionally stolen until the real baby is brought back to her. Monster Is a Mommy: She starts terrorizing Jollywood after the dwarfs find a pearl, inspiring Hildy to turn her husband, Grim, into a dragon to scare the villagers. She then takes Dragon!Grim into her cave. Turns out, said pearl is the dragon's egg and the mother dragon has mistaken Dragon!Grim for her lost baby.
